summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 10: re-detect against main (66 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (72 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 10 detection: 6 from clusters = 6 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 10 verify: 6 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: packages/table/src/Filters/Constraints/NumberConstraint.php:33,64 + DateConstraint.php:35,68 (apply throws); reached unguarded from QueryBuilderFilter::applyConditions packages/table/src/Filters/QueryBuilderFilter.php:142; value sourced from request in TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters packages/table/src/TableQueryBuilder.php:75-86
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: QueryBuilderFilter constraints throw InvalidArgumentException on malformed user-supplied values -> uncaught HTTP 500 (DoS)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Wrap the per-leaf `$constraint->apply(...)` call in QueryBuilderFilter::applyConditions (line 142) in a try/catch(InvalidArgumentException) that silently `continue`s (drop the malformed condition), matching the filter's documented "silently dropped" / graceful-degradation contract; OR make Number/Date constraints no-op instead of throwing on bad value shape. Add a regression test feeding a whitelisted field+operator with a malformed value and asserting no exception.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely. The QueryBuilderFilter whitelist (QueryBuilderFilter.php:128-140) validates only the condition's `field` and `operator`, never the value SHAPE. `$condition['value']` flows straight from request input (TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters line 75 -> Filter::applyToQuery -> QueryBuilderFilter::applyConditions line 142). NumberConstraint::apply throws InvalidArgumentException when a `between` value is not a 2-element array (line 33) or when castNumeric receives a non-numeric value (line 64); DateConstraint throws identically (lines 35,68,76). No try/catch wraps the call in QueryBuilderFilter, and the upstream try/catch in InertiaDataBuilder::runTableQueryBuilder (lines 431-436) only guards reflection instantiation — `$instance->build()` at line 442 (which triggers applyFilters) is OUTSIDE it. So the exception propagates uncaught to the Inertia request middleware -> HTTP 500. I wrote a Pest repro using a whitelisted field `id` (NumberConstraint) with whitelisted operators: payload {field:'id', operator:'between', value:'not-an-array'} and {field:'id', operator:'>', value:'abc'} — both ->throws(InvalidArgumentException) assertions passed through the public applyToQuery entry point. The field+operator are both whitelisted, so the documented whitelist does not stop these; only the value shape is malformed. This is a framework bug, not app-misuse: a consumer declaring QueryBuilderFilter exactly as documented in PLANNING/09 §VQB (lines 4067-4074) is exposed, and the malformed input arrives via an attacker-crafted ?filter[...] querystring that bypasses any React-side value input. It contradicts the framework's own stated design (QueryBuilderFilter.php:18-20 'silently dropped' / graceful degradation, mirroring TableQueryBuilder's graceful handling of bad sort/search/per_page). Severity capped at medium (not high/blocker): it is an authenticated per-request 500 that fails fast (no real availability DoS, no data corruption, no injection, no unauth/data-leak), and apps/showcase — the baseline — does not currently declare any QueryBuilderFilter, so this is a latent robustness gap reachable only once a consumer opts into the VQB filter, not a live exploit in the shipped baseline. The 'DoS' framing in the report is overstated. Not the injection vector itself (field/operator whitelisting works); it is a value-shape robustness defect.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: QueryBuilderFilter documents a 'Security guarantee: any field that does not match a declared constraint is silently dropped' and the design intent is graceful degradation (TableQueryBuilder excludes/ignores bad input elsewhere). But the whitelist only validates the condition's `field` and `operator` (QueryBuilderFilter.php:132-140) — NOT the value SHAPE. The value (`$condition['value']`) comes straight from request input (`?filter[q][...]`) via TableQueryBuilder::applyFilters (line 75: `$this->request->input('filter', [])`) -> Filter::applyToQuery. NumberConstraint::apply throws InvalidArgumentException for a `between` operator whose value is not a 2-element array (line 33) and for any operator whose value is non-numeric (castNumeric, line 64). DateConstraint throws identically (lines 35, 68, 76). Neither QueryBuilderFilter::applyToQuery (line 74) nor applyConditions (line 142) wraps the call in try/catch, so the exception propagates to an unhandled HTTP 500. Live repro (showcase, NumberConstraint('id') on a QueryBuilderFilter): payload {operator:'AND', conditions:[{field:'id', operator:'between', value:'not-an-array'}]} -> THREW InvalidArgumentException 'NumberConstraint::between expects array [min, max], got string'; payload {field:'id', operator:'>', value:'abc'} -> THREW 'NumberConstraint expects numeric value, got abc'. Both use a whitelisted field AND whitelisted operator, so the documented whitelist does not stop them. Any authenticated user viewing a table that declares a QueryBuilderFilter can trigger a 500 with a crafted filter querystring. Spec (PLANNING/09 §VQB) demands 'Serialização -> SQL safe' and a 'Security test: injection' but the value-shape robustness path was never hardened — constraints should drop/no-op (or 422) on malformed values rather than throw an uncaught 500.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: State-transition history writer keys model_type by raw FQCN, breaking the documented stateTransitions() relation under a morph map
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In PersistStateTransitionToHistory::handle(), write 'model_type' => $event->record->getMorphClass() instead of $event->record::class, so it matches HasWorkflow::stateTransitions() morphMany (which queries getMorphClass()). Also update StateTransitionField::resolveHistory() (src/Fields/StateTransitionField.php:243) to query ->where('model_type', $this->record->getMorphClass()) for consistency, mirroring how arqel-dev/versioning uses versionable()->associate()/getMorphClass().
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced live in packages/workflow against its own fixtures. With Relation::enforceMorphMap(['wf_order' => FreeFormWorkflowOrder::class]) and a transition, the test asserted (4 passing assertions): getMorphClass()='wf_order'; persisted model_type=FQCN (Arqel\Workflow\Tests\Fixtures\FreeFormWorkflowOrder); direct FQCN query returns 1 row (history WAS written); but $order->stateTransitions()->count()=0. The documented append-only relation HasWorkflow::stateTransitions() is morphMany(StateTransition::class,'model'), which filters model_type=getMorphClass() (the alias), while the writer PersistStateTransitionToHistory::handle() persists $event->record::class (raw FQCN). Under an enforced morph map these diverge, so the relation silently returns 0 rows. The StateTransitionField::resolveHistory() read path queries by $this->record::class (FQCN) so it stays self-consistent with the writer today, but it too would break once the writer is corrected to the alias — both sites must move to getMorphClass(). Contrast verified: arqel-dev/versioning writes via $version->versionable()->associate($model) (Eloquent-native, stores the alias) and queries getMorphClass(), so its versions() morphMany works correctly under the same morph map. This is a genuine framework correctness defect (writer bypasses Eloquent morph handling), not app misuse and not a deferred feature. Severity is medium rather than high because it is latent: it only manifests when the app calls Relation::enforceMorphMap, which apps/showcase does not currently do, so no current shipped consumer hits it; but morph maps are a standard Laravel practice and the documented stateTransitions() relation is the public contract, so it is a real ship-blocker for any morph-map-using consumer.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: PersistStateTransitionToHistory::handle() persists `StateTransition::create(['model_type' => $event->record::class, ...])` (raw FQCN), but the documented append-only history relation HasWorkflow::stateTransitions() is a `morphMany(StateTransition::class, 'model')` which queries `model_type = getMorphClass()`. With an enforced morph map these diverge: the FQCN is written, the alias is queried, so the relation silently returns 0 rows even though history WAS persisted. Live repro on Ticket: after `Relation::enforceMorphMap(['ticket' => Ticket::class])` and a transition, `getMorphClass()='ticket'`, written `model_type='App\\Models\\Ticket'`, `$t->stateTransitions()->count()=0` while a direct query by `::class` returns 1. Contrast: arqel-dev/versioning uses the Eloquent-native `versionable()->associate($model)` and correctly writes the alias (`versionable_type='post'`), so its `versions()` relation works under the same morph map (verified live). Fix: writer (packages/workflow/src/Listeners/PersistStateTransitionToHistory.php) and StateTransitionField::resolveHistory() (packages/workflow/src/Fields/StateTransitionField.php, queries by `$this->record::class`) should both use `getMorphClass()` to match morphMany. No morph-map handling exists anywhere in the workflow package (grep for enforceMorphMap/getMorphClass/morphMap in src+tests returns nothing), so this is uncovered. File: packages/workflow/src/Listeners/PersistStateTransitionToHistory.php

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: DateRangeFilter default serializes raw DateTimeImmutable (PHP-cast object), but the React DateRangeControl only accepts ISO strings — the declared default range is silently dropped on the client
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In DateRangeFilter add `protected function resolveDefault(): mixed { return ['from' => $this->default['from']?->format(DATE_ATOM), 'to' => $this->default['to']?->format(DATE_ATOM)]; }` (handle null default), and have Dashboard::filters() store the ISO-formatted default into the `filters` seed (call $entry->resolveDefault()-equivalent instead of raw getDefault()) so the seeded values are strings too. Also update FilterTest.php:22 to assert ISO strings. Note: input type=date needs YYYY-MM-DD, so consider formatting to 'Y-m-d' rather than DATE_ATOM for the consumer to populate cleanly.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end at the framework boundary. (1) DateRangeFilter::defaultRange() stores raw DateTimeInterface objects and does NOT override Filter::resolveDefault(), which the base class explicitly documents subclasses "should override" for DateTimeInterface to ISO 8601 (Filter.php:123-131). (2) Empirically confirmed plain DateTimeImmutable json_encodes to {"date":"2026-01-01 00:00:00.000000","timezone_type":3,"timezone":"UTC"} (it is NOT JsonSerializable), exactly matching the evidence; only Carbon emits an ISO string. (3) Ran DateRangeFilter::make('range')->defaultRange(new DateTimeImmutable(...),...)->toArray() and got default.from/default.to as the nested PHP-cast object, not a string. (4) The React consumer DashboardFilters.tsx:143-144 does `typeof range.from === 'string' ? range.from : ''`, so an object value falls back to empty — the declared default date range silently never populates the <input type=date> controls on initial render. (5) Dashboard::resolve() emits the same raw default in its `filters` seed (Dashboard.php:314 via filters() lines 186-191), and WidgetRenderer.withFilters() (WidgetRenderer.tsx:135) serializes via String(value), mangling the object into query params for deferred/poll fetches. (6) The existing PHP test FilterTest.php:22 asserts the buggy shape (->toBe(['from'=>$from,'to'=>$to]) with raw objects), so it passes while the cross-boundary contract is broken. This is a framework-internal contract violation — the framework's own React consumer cannot read what the framework's own public API serializes — not app misuse. Classified framework-bug. Severity medium not higher: DateRangeFilter is a Phase-2 declarative-filter feature NOT actually used in the shipping apps/showcase (only symfony's unrelated DateRangeFilterIterator matched), it degrades gracefully (empty inputs, no crash/error), and a workaround exists (pass Carbon or pre-format via ->default()). The 'live repro in apps/showcase' in the evidence is hypothetical — showcase does not wire this filter — but the framework defect itself is concretely reproduced.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/widgets/src/Filters/DateRangeFilter.php: defaultRange(?DateTimeInterface $from, ?DateTimeInterface $to) stores `$this->default = ['from'=>$from,'to'=>$to]` with raw DateTimeInterface objects and does NOT override Filter::resolveDefault(). The Filter base (packages/widgets/src/Filters/Filter.php:123-131) explicitly documents: "Subclasses that need to massage the default (e.g. DateTimeInterface ↦ ISO 8601 string) should override [resolveDefault()]" — but DateRangeFilter never does. Live repro in apps/showcase: Dashboard::make()->filters([DateRangeFilter::make('range')->defaultRange(new DateTimeImmutable('2026-01-01'), new DateTimeImmutable('2026-01-31'))])->resolve(null) emits both the dashboard `filters` seed AND the declared-filter `default` as nested {"from":{"date":"2026-01-01 00:00:00.000000","timezone_type":3,"timezone":"UTC"},"to":{...}} — a PHP DateTime-cast object, NOT a string (DateTimeImmutable is not JsonSerializable; only Carbon emits ISO). The consumer packages-js/ui/src/widgets/DashboardFilters.tsx DateRangeControl does: `const fromValue = typeof range.from === 'string' ? range.from : ''` (same for `to`). Because the value is an object not a string, both `<input type="date">` controls fall back to empty, so a dashboard's declared default date range never populates the inputs on initial render. Secondary fallout: this raw-DateTime value is also what WidgetDataController seeds into Widget::filterValue('range') for deferred/poll fetches, and WidgetRenderer.tsx withFilters() serializes filter values via String(value), which mangles the object in `filters[range]=...` query params. The PHP unit test packages/widgets/tests/Unit/Filters/FilterTest.php asserts the buggy shape (->toBe(['from'=>$from,'to'=>$to]) with raw objects), so it passes while the cross-boundary contract is broken. Fix: override resolveDefault() in DateRangeFilter to format endpoints to ISO 8601 strings (e.g. ['from'=>$from?->format(DATE_ATOM),'to'=>$to?->format(DATE_ATOM)]) AND have Dashboard::resolve() emit the formatted defaults in its `filters` seed.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: packages/fields/src/Types/FileField.php:164-171 getDefaultRules() returns ['array'] for multiple; getNestedValidationRules() not overridden (base returns []); contrast SelectField.php:222-235 which DOES emit {name}.* nested rules for multiple
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Multiple FileField/ImageField emits no per-element validation rules — maxSize/mimetypes/image silently dropped on the main form submit
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Override getNestedValidationRules() in FileField to return ['{name}.*' => array_merge(['nullable'], $this->uploadFileRules())] when $this->multiple, mirroring SelectField.php:222-235; ImageField inherits image-gated element rules via its uploadFileRules() override.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced live against packages/fields/src + apps/showcase on current branch. (new FileField('docs'))->multiple()->maxSize(1024)->acceptedFileTypes(['application/pdf']) yields getValidationRules()=['nullable','array'], getNestedValidationRules()=[], and FieldRulesExtractor->extract([$f])={'docs':['nullable','array']} with NO 'docs.*' element rule. FileField::getDefaultRules() (FileField.php:166-168) short-circuits to ['array'] for multiple and never overrides getNestedValidationRules() (base Field.php:211 returns []). The existing uploadFileRules() (FileField.php:222-235, image-gated override in ImageField.php:59-72) is only invoked by uploadRule() on the SINGLE-file path, so the configured maxSize/mimetypes/image checks are dropped for the multiple main-payload submit. FieldRulesExtractor.php:39-41 already merges any getNestedValidationRules() under its dotted path, and SelectField.php:222-235 uses exactly this mechanism for multiple selects — so the framework has the precedent and FileField diverges. This is a genuine framework bug, not app misuse or intended/deferred behavior. Severity low: the default 'direct' strategy validates each file per-upload via FieldUploadController::buildFileRules() (required|file|max|mimetypes, Controllers/FieldUploadController.php:211-224) and the form re-submits stored path strings, so the mainstream flow is protected; the gap only affects a non-direct strategy or any flow posting raw files in the main payload — defense-in-depth rather than an exploitable hole in the shipped default. Single-file path is unaffected (uploadRule() closure runs real checks).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: FileField::getDefaultRules() returns just `['array']` when `multiple` is set, and FileField does NOT override getNestedValidationRules(), so the base Field returns []. Live repro (showcase): `(new FileField('docs'))->multiple()->maxSize(1024)->acceptedFileTypes(['application/pdf'])` -> getValidationRules() = ['nullable','array']; getNestedValidationRules() = []; FieldRulesExtractor::extract([$f]) = ['docs' => ['nullable','array']]. The configured maxSize and acceptedFileTypes never become validation rules for the main create/update payload — there is no `docs.*` element rule for `file`/`max`/`mimetypes`. This is the exact pattern SelectField got right (SelectField.php:222-235 emits `{name}.*` => [in:...] for multiple selects), so the framework already has the precedent and FileField diverges. Mitigating context: the documented `direct` upload strategy routes each file through FieldUploadController::store, which DOES validate `required|file|max|mimetypes` per file (FieldUploadController.php:211-226), so in the mainstream flow files are validated at upload time and the form merely re-submits stored paths. Severity is therefore defense-in-depth: a non-direct strategy (or any flow that posts raw files in the main payload) accepts arbitrarily large files of any type for a multiple field. PLANNING/08 FIELDS upload AC explicitly lists 'upload single/multiple, validation' as required coverage.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldUploadController.php:211-226 buildFileRules() always uses ['required','file',...]; ImageField::uploadFileRules() packages/fields/src/Types/ImageField.php:59-72 returns ['image',...] but is protected and never consulted by the controller
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: FieldUploadController hard-codes 'file' for ImageField uploads instead of consuming ImageField::uploadFileRules() ('image') — image content not validated at the real upload entry point
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make uploadFileRules() public on FileField and have buildFileRules() delegate to $field->uploadFileRules() (then prepend 'required'), so the HTTP upload path honors ImageField's 'image' rule exactly like the main-form uploadRule() closure does.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed by source inspection. ImageField::uploadFileRules() (ImageField.php:59-72) overrides FileField::uploadFileRules() (FileField.php:222-235) to gate real uploads on Laravel's 'image' rule (which decodes the file to verify it is a genuine image) instead of the bare 'file' rule. The main-form validation path consumes this polymorphically: FileField::getDefaultRules()->uploadRule() closure calls $this->uploadFileRules() (FileField.php:207). But the live HTTP upload endpoint, FieldUploadController::store() -> buildFileRules() (FieldUploadController.php:61-62, 211-226), re-implements rule construction with a hard-coded ['required','file'] for ALL FileField subclasses including ImageField, and never calls uploadFileRules(). So an ImageField direct-upload is gated only on 'file' + the mimetypes whitelist, never on 'image'. This is a genuine producer/consumer divergence: two validation paths for the same field disagree. The practical impact is bounded because ImageField defaults acceptedFileTypes to image mimes, so the controller still emits mimetypes:image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp (content-based MIME detection rejects renamed non-images in the default config). The reproducible gap appears when a developer calls ->acceptedFileTypes([]) on an ImageField: buildFileRules() then skips the mimetypes rule (FieldUploadController.php:221, since $accepted===[]), leaving only ['required','file'], so any non-image file is accepted as an image upload via the HTTP endpoint, while the main-form closure path would still enforce 'image'. No test in FieldUploadControllerTest.php exercises the image-rule path or the empty-acceptedFileTypes case, so this is not asserted intended behavior; it is an oversight, not a deferred feature (unrelated to EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL items). Severity low: requires an atypical explicit ->acceptedFileTypes([]) config, impact is storing a non-image as an 'image' (no execution / no path traversal — destroy() is separately path-guarded), and the inconsistency itself is the real defect rather than a security hole.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: ImageField defines its own upload validation in uploadFileRules() returning ['image', max:..., mimetypes:...] (ImageField.php:61) — the `image` rule actually decodes the file to confirm it is a real image. But the live upload endpoint, FieldUploadController::buildFileRules (line 213), re-implements rule construction with a hard-coded `['required','file']` for BOTH FileField and ImageField and never calls the field's uploadFileRules(). So an ImageField upload is gated only on `file` + the `mimetypes` whitelist, never on `image`. This is the recurring meta-class 'a field-defined rule that exists but is never consumed at the real entry point' (uploadFileRules is the field's canonical upload-rule source for the main-form closure path in FileField::uploadRule, yet the HTTP upload controller diverges). Practical gap is bounded because ImageField defaults acceptedFileTypes to image mimes, so `mimetypes:image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp` still applies — but a developer who calls `->acceptedFileTypes([])` on an ImageField clears that gate, leaving only `file`, so any non-image file is accepted as an 'image' upload, while the main-form closure path (FileField::uploadRule -> uploadFileRules) would have enforced `image`. Producer/consumer mismatch between the field's declared upload rules and the controller that should honor them.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: audit_user config documented to accept a Closure is silently ignored — resolver guards on is_string()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Replace `if (is_string($resolver) && $resolver !== '' && is_callable($resolver))` with `if (is_callable($resolver))` so Closure and array callables documented in config are honored (empty string is not callable, so the guard is subsumed).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced at code level. config/arqel-versioning.php lines 43-46 explicitly document audit_user as accepting a "Closure registered via container" with the stated use case being CLI/queue jobs where Auth is not hydrated. But Versionable::resolveAuditUserId() (line 240) gates the resolver on `is_string($resolver) && $resolver !== '' && is_callable($resolver)`. Verified with PHP that a Closure (fn() => 42) and an array callable [$obj,'method'] are is_callable() true but is_string() false, so both fail the guard and the code falls through to Auth::id() (line 247) — which returns null in exactly the documented CLI/queue scenario. Only string FQCN::method callables ('strlen', 'App\\Foo::bar') pass. The existing tests (VersioningCoverageGapsTest) only exercise string-FQCN resolvers, so the gap is untested. This is a real divergence between documented and implemented behavior (documented Closure path silently ignored), classifying as a framework bug. Severity is low rather than medium: created_by_user_id is audit metadata only (no security/correctness impact on data), the value is read via config() so it is reachable only by an app that publishes config or calls config()->set() with a Closure (the default env() path always yields a string and is unaffected), and the failure mode is a degraded fallback to Auth::id()/null rather than an exception. Not a deferred feature and not app-misuse — the app would be following the documented contract.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: config/arqel-versioning.php documents `audit_user` as a "Callable (FQCN::method ou Closure registered via container)". But Versionable::resolveAuditUserId() gates the resolver with `if (is_string($resolver) && $resolver !== '' && is_callable($resolver))`. A Closure (or array callable `[$obj,'method']`) is `is_callable` but NOT `is_string`, so it is silently dropped and the code falls through to `Auth::id()` — meaning the documented Closure path never resolves the configured user (e.g. for CLI/queue jobs where Auth is not hydrated, which is exactly the documented use case). Probe confirmed: a `fn() => 42` resolver takes the 'Closure IGNORED' branch; only string callables ('strlen', 'App\\Foo::bar') pass. Reachable via direct config-array override (env() always yields a string so env path is unaffected). Fix: drop the `is_string()` requirement, gate on `is_callable($resolver)` alone. File: packages/versioning/src/Concerns/Versionable.php (resolveAuditUserId)
